exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Longer femurs require 30-35° torso lean. Bar on upper traps (NOT lower back). Drive through mid-foot. Keep vertical shin angle to reduce knee shear. Maintain neutral spine (L4-L5 protection).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps hypertrophy. Noa: L4-L5 risk if torso angle >35°. Boris: Top set + 2 back-off sets.

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.setupNotes: More upright torso (20-25°) reduces L4-L5 shear. Bar on front delts. Longer femurs benefit from heeled shoes. Elbows high, core braced.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, quad-dominant. Noa: Safer for lower back vs back squat. Boris: Moderate weight, focus form.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Hold DB at chest, forces upright torso. Excellent for taller lifters. Elbow inside knees at bottom. Minimal spinal load.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, great warm-up. Noa: Very safe for L4-L5. Boris: Pump work, slow tempo.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Longer stride length needed. Front shin vertical. Torso slightly forward (15-20°). DBs at sides, minimal spinal compression.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each leg. Noa: Excellent unilateral, safe for back. Boris: Build to heavy, then drop sets.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.setupNotes: Feet high on platform (reduces hip flexion strain). Lower back flat against pad. Don't round lower back at bottom (L4-L5 safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps, high volume. Noa: Safe quad/glute builder if ROM controlled. Boris: Pyramids, partial reps at top.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Fixed angle reduces torque variability. Shoulders against pads. Knees track over toes. Vertical shin reduces patellar stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, quad emphasis. Noa: Lower back supported. Boris: Great for intensity techniques.

exercises.name: Safety Bar Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Bar weight forward shifts torso more upright. Reduces shoulder/elbow strain (medial epicondylitis safety). Maintains L4-L5 neutrality better than back squat.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-10 reps. Noa: Best squat variant for elbow/shoulder injuries. Boris: Build to heavy singles.

exercises.name: Box Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Box height at parallel or slightly above. Sit back aggressively (longer femurs). Pause on box, explode up. Teaches posterior chain.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-6 reps, power focus. Noa: Controlled depth protects L4-L5. Boris: Speed work, 50-70% 1RM.

exercises.name: Pause Squat
exercises.setupNotes: 2-3 second pause at bottom. Longer femurs = more quad demand. Maintain brace, don't relax at bottom (L4-L5). Builds bottom strength.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 4-8 reps, strength phase. Noa: Great for teaching depth control. Boris: 70-85% 1RM pauses.

exercises.name: Sumo Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Wide stance reduces forward lean. Toes out 30-45°. Knees track over toes. Reduces hip flexion demand (beneficial for taller lifters).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, glute/adductor emphasis. Noa: Alternative if conventional hurts. Boris: Moderate weight, volume.

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Longer femurs = higher hip start position. Hinge at hips, NOT round back. Neutral spine critical (L4-L5). Bar over mid-foot. Pull slack out first.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps. Noa: HIGH L4-L5 RISK - monitor closely. Boris: Build singles, limit volume.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge pattern. Longer torso = greater hamstring stretch. Keep bar close to shins. Slight knee bend. NEVER round lower back (L4-L5 priority).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, hamstring hypertrophy. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk, watch form. Boris: Controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Neutral grip (elbow-safe). More upright torso vs conventional. Reduced L4-L5 shear. Handles allow longer range for taller lifters.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-10 reps, safer alternative. Noa: MUCH safer for L4-L5 and elbows. Boris: Great for high-rep work.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Wide stance reduces forward lean (L4-L5 benefit). Hips closer to bar. Toes out. Activates adductors. Shorter ROM for tall lifters.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps. Noa: Better L4-L5 loading vs conventional. Boris: Test max variation.

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
exercises.setupNotes: Longer levers = greater balance challenge. DB in opposite hand. Slight standing knee bend. Hinge from hip, NOT spine rotation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each leg. Noa: Minimal spinal load, safe unilateral. Boris: Stability work.

exercises.name: Deficit Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Stand on 1-2" platform. Increased ROM = greater posterior chain demand. CRITICAL: Maintain neutral spine (L4-L5 risk at deeper depth).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-6 reps, strength work. Noa: HIGH RISK - only with perfect form. Boris: Overload stretch, careful.

exercises.name: Rack Pull
exercises.setupNotes: Bar at knee height. Reduced ROM = heavier load. Targets upper back/traps. Less L4-L5 stress than conventional.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps, overload. Noa: Safer partial ROM for back. Boris: Supramax loading.

exercises.name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift
exercises.setupNotes: Legs almost straight (slight knee unlock). Greater hamstring stretch. Bar stays close. CRITICAL: Neutral spine always.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, hamstring focus. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk. Boris: Light-moderate weight.

exercises.name: Barbell Flat Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Longer arms = longer ROM (strength disadvantage). Arch back, retract scapulae. Bar to lower chest. Elbows 45° (NOT 90° - elbow safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps. Noa: 45° elbow angle protects medial epicondyle. Boris: Comp lift, build technique.

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: 30° bench angle. Upper chest emphasis. Bar to upper chest/clavicle area. Same 45° elbow angle for elbow safety.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, clavicular pec. Noa: Elbow-safe pressing angle. Boris: Moderate weight, volume.

exercises.name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: 15-30° decline. Lower chest emphasis. Shorter ROM vs flat (strength advantage). Bar to lower sternum.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-10 reps, lower pec. Noa: Reduced shoulder stress. Boris: Heavy pressing variation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Flat Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Greater ROM than barbell (stretch advantage). Natural arc path. Elbows 45°. Neutral wrist (elbow safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps, hypertrophy. Noa: Safer for elbows than barbell. Boris: Drop sets, volume work.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: 30-45° angle. DBs allow greater stretch. Arc toward midline at top. Shoulder-friendly path.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, upper chest. Noa: Excellent for elbow health. Boris: Pyramids, slow eccentric.

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly
exercises.setupNotes: Longer arms = greater stretch. Handles at chest height. Slight elbow bend (NOT straight - elbow protection). Squeeze pecs at center.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, isolation. Noa: Very elbow-safe, constant tension. Boris: Pump work, drop sets.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
exercises.setupNotes: Adjust seat so handles at mid-chest. Fixed path reduces stabilizer demand. Good for high-rep work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, supported. Boris: Finisher, pump sets.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench Press
exercises.setupNotes: Hands shoulder-width. Tricep emphasis. Elbows close to body (elbow safety). Bar to lower chest.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps, triceps. Noa: Monitor elbow - may aggravate medial epicondylitis. Boris: Accessory for lockout.

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.setupNotes: Hands slightly wider than shoulders. Elbows 45°. Torso rigid. Full ROM (chest to floor).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-30 reps, endurance. Noa: Safe pressing, scalable. Boris: Warm-up, finisher.

exercises.name: Barbell Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge position. Torso 30-45° angle. Pull to lower sternum. Maintain neutral spine (L4-L5). Supinated grip may stress elbow.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps, lat/rhomboid. Noa: L4-L5 risk if form breaks, elbow stress with supination. Boris: Build strength, moderate volume.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hand on bench, torso parallel. Pull DB to hip. Neutral grip (elbow-safe). Don't rotate torso. Control eccentric.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each side. Noa: Excellent for elbow safety, unilateral. Boris: Moderate-heavy, strict form.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Row
exercises.setupNotes: Incline bench removes L4-L5 loading. Chest supported. Pull handles to sides. Focus on scapular retraction.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: SAFEST rowing - no L4-L5 stress. Boris: Volume work, pump.

exercises.name: Cable Row
exercises.setupNotes: Seated, chest up. Pull handle to sternum. Elbows back. Neutral spine. Controlled return (constant tension).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, mid-back. Noa: L4-L5 safe if upright. Boris: Drop sets, time under tension.

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge, chest up. Pull bar to sternum. Neutral grip (elbow-safe). Similar L4-L5 demands to barbell row.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk, better elbow position. Boris: Heavy rowing variation.

exercises.name: Seal Row
exercises.setupNotes: Prone on bench. Bar underneath. Pull to bench. Removes L4-L5 entirely. Pure lat/rhomboid work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: SAFEST heavy rowing - zero spinal load. Boris: Strength work without fatigue.

exercises.name: Pendlay Row
exercises.setupNotes: Bar starts from floor each rep. Explosive pull. Torso near parallel. Reset each rep. High L4-L5 demand.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-8 reps, power. Noa: HIGH L4-L5 RISK - only if healthy. Boris: Strength/power phase.

exercises.name: Inverted Row
exercises.setupNotes: Under bar at waist height. Body rigid. Pull chest to bar. Elbows back. Scalable with bar height.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, no equipment. Boris: Warm-up, endurance.

exercises.name: Standing Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.setupNotes: Taller height = longer bar path. Bar starts at clavicle. Press straight up. Core braced (L4-L5 stability). Elbows forward at start.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-10 reps, strength. Noa: Shoulder/elbow stress - monitor. Boris: Top set + back-offs.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Overhead Press
exercises.setupNotes: Seated removes leg drive. DBs allow natural arc. Press up and slightly together. Back supported (L4-L5 safe).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: Safer than standing, supported spine. Boris: Hypertrophy work.

exercises.name: Arnold Press
exercises.setupNotes: Start palms facing you. Rotate while pressing. Hits all three delt heads. Controlled rotation (elbow safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, complete delt. Noa: Monitor elbow during rotation. Boris: Moderate weight, pump.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.setupNotes: Adjust seat height. Handles start at shoulder level. Fixed path. Good for high-rep work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, supported. Boris: Finisher, metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Landmine Press
exercises.setupNotes: Arc path easier on shoulders. One-arm or two-arm. Stand or half-kneel. Reduced shoulder stress vs vertical press.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Shoulder-friendly angle. Boris: Great accessory.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Hips high, inverted V position. Head toward floor. Mimics overhead press pattern. Scalable bodyweight work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Safe pressing pattern. Boris: Warm-up, finisher.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Pronated grip, shoulder-width+. Full ROM (chest to bar). Scapulae depressed. Longer arms = greater challenge.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 5-15 reps, lat hypertrophy. Noa: Elbow-safe with pronated grip. Boris: Weight added if >12 reps.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Supinated grip, shoulder-width. Bicep emphasis. Full ROM. Monitor elbow (supination stress on medial epicondyle).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-12 reps, bicep/lat. Noa: May aggravate elbow injury. Boris: Build to weighted.

exercises.name: Neutral Grip Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Palms facing each other. Most elbow-friendly grip. Balanced lat/bicep. Excellent for injury prevention.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-15 reps. Noa: BEST grip for elbow safety. Boris: Primary variation for high volume.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Wide grip, pronated. Pull bar to upper chest. Slight lean back. Squeeze lats. Scalable resistance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, lat width. Noa: Safe alternative to pull-ups. Boris: Drop sets, volume.

exercises.name: Close-Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Narrow V-bar or close handles. Pull to sternum. Lat thickness emphasis. Elbow-friendly.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, mid-back. Noa: Very safe grip. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.setupNotes: Arms nearly straight. Pull down in arc to hips. Lat isolation. Slight elbow bend (safety).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, isolation. Noa: Safe lat work. Boris: Pre-exhaust or finisher.

exercises.name: Assisted Pull-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Machine counterbalances bodyweight. Build toward unassisted. Maintain full ROM and form.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: Good for building strength safely. Boris: Progress to bodyweight.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Prone on bench. Pad at ankles. Curl heels to glutes. Hip stability critical. Don't arch lower back (L4-L5).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps, hamstring isolation. Noa: Safe, no spinal load. Boris: Drop sets, pump.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Hip flexed position. Greater hamstring stretch. Pad behind calves. Control eccentric.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Very safe, supported. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Nordic Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Kneeling, ankles anchored. Lower torso slowly (eccentric). VERY challenging. Build tolerance gradually.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps, strength. Noa: Injury prevention gold standard. Boris: Negatives if can't do full.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.setupNotes: Pad at shins. Extend fully. Quad isolation. Avoid if knee pain.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, quad isolation. Noa: Can stress patella - monitor knees. Boris: Pre-exhaust or finisher.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Full ROM (deep stretch to full contraction). Toes on platform edge. Knee straight for gastrocnemius emphasis.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-30 reps, high volume. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Straight sets, pump.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Knee bent 90°. Targets soleus (deep calf). Full ROM.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-30 reps. Noa: Safe, seated. Boris: Superset with standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Supinated grip. Elbows at sides. Curl to shoulders. Controlled eccentric. Monitor elbow (medial epicondyle stress).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: May aggravate elbow injury. Boris: Moderate weight, strict form.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Alternating or simultaneous. Can supinate during curl. Neutral at bottom is elbow-friendlier.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Safer than barbell for elbows. Boris: Volume work.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Neutral grip (palms facing). Targets brachioradialis. SAFEST curl for elbow health.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: BEST curl for medial epicondylitis. Boris: Primary bicep work.

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Constant tension. Low pulley. Curl handle to shoulders. Elbows stable.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, pump. Noa: Moderate elbow stress. Boris: Finisher, drop sets.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown
exercises.setupNotes: High pulley. Rope or bar. Elbows at sides. Press down fully. Don't let elbows flare.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Monitor elbow - moderate stress. Boris: Drop sets, pump.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.setupNotes: DB overhead. Lower behind head. Keep elbows close. Full stretch. Shoulder mobility required.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps, long head. Noa: Elbow/shoulder stress - monitor. Boris: Moderate weight.

exercises.name: Skull Crusher
exercises.setupNotes: Lying flat. Lower bar to forehead/behind head. Elbows stay in. HIGH elbow stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: HIGH elbow risk - avoid if injured. Boris: EZ-bar reduces strain.

exercises.name: Dips
exercises.setupNotes: Lean forward for chest emphasis. Vertical for triceps. Full ROM. Monitor shoulder/elbow.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 6-15 reps. Noa: High shoulder/elbow stress. Boris: Weight added if >12 reps.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at sides. Raise laterally to shoulder height. Slight elbow bend. Lead with elbows. Controlled descent.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, side delts. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: High rep, pump work.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.setupNotes: Rope at face height. Pull to forehead. External rotation. Rear delt/rotator cuff health.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: ESSENTIAL for shoulder health. Boris: Every session.

exercises.name: Rear Delt Fly
exercises.setupNotes: Bent over or chest-supported. DBs to sides in arc. Squeeze rear delts. Maintain neutral spine.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Safe, posture-improving. Boris: High rep work.

exercises.name: Shrugs
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at sides. Elevate shoulders (NOT roll). Squeeze traps at top. Controlled descent.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps, trap mass. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Heavy weight, volume.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.setupNotes: Forearms on ground. Body rigid. Neutral spine (L4-L5 protection). Hold time over reps.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 30-90 sec holds. Noa: CRITICAL for L4-L5 stability. Boris: Every session.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.setupNotes: Knees on ground (or standing advanced). Roll forward, maintain neutral spine. Don't let back arch (L4-L5 risk).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: Advanced - only if core strong. Boris: Build to standing.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.setupNotes: High pulley, rope. Kneeling. Crunch down (flex spine). Abs do the work, not hip flexors.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps, ab mass. Noa: Safe weighted ab work. Boris: Progressive overload.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.setupNotes: Seated, lean back. Rotate torso side to side. Optional weight. Don't round spine (L4-L5).
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-40 reps (total). Noa: Obliques, moderate back stress. Boris: Light weight, high rep.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Hang from bar. Raise legs (bent or straight). Abs do lifting, NOT momentum. Lower abs emphasis.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Grip/shoulder demand. Boris: Build to L-sit holds.

exercises.name: Farmer's Walk
exercises.setupNotes: Heavy DBs at sides. Walk with neutral spine. Core braced. Grip endurance + core stability.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 30-60 sec walks. Noa: Excellent functional strength. Boris: Heavy, short distance.

exercises.name: Sled Push
exercises.setupNotes: Lean forward, drive through legs. Minimal L4-L5 compression. Excellent conditioning.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-60 sec pushes. Noa: Very safe, no eccentric damage. Boris: Finisher or conditioning.

exercises.name: Sled Pull
exercises.setupNotes: Backward walk or row. Rope/strap. Posterior chain emphasis. Safe high-volume work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-60 sec pulls. Noa: Safe, no eccentric. Boris: Conditioning between sets.

exercises.name: Battle Ropes
exercises.setupNotes: Athletic stance. Waves, slams, or circles. Full body conditioning. Core braced.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-40 sec rounds. Noa: Safe conditioning. Boris: Finisher or warm-up.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.setupNotes: Explosive jump onto box. Soft landing. Step down (DON'T jump down - reduces injury risk). Power development.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-8 reps, explosive. Noa: Land softly, knee protection. Boris: Early session, fresh CNS.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slam
exercises.setupNotes: Ball overhead, slam to ground. Full body power. Pick up and repeat. Great for power endurance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Safe explosive work. Boris: Conditioning or warm-up.

exercises.name: Broad Jump
exercises.setupNotes: Horizontal jump for distance. Arm swing for momentum. Land softly. Triple extension power.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-6 reps. Noa: Land correctly - knee safety. Boris: Power testing.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.setupNotes: Hip hinge pattern. Explosive hip drive. KB to chest height. Posterior chain power. L4-L5 demands if form breaks.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Moderate L4-L5 risk. Boris: Power endurance.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Lying to standing with KB overhead. Complex movement. Shoulder stability + core control. Learn progressions first.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 3-5 reps each side. Noa: Shoulder/core strength. Boris: Skill work, light weight.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.setupNotes: Upper back on bench. Bar across hips. Drive through heels. Squeeze glutes. Minimal L4-L5 stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps, glute hypertrophy. Noa: Very safe for L4-L5. Boris: Heavy, progressive.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.setupNotes: Same as glute bridge but greater ROM. Bar across hips. Full hip extension. Glute activation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: Safe, glute-dominant. Boris: Build to heavy loads.

exercises.name: Wall Sit
exercises.setupNotes: Back against wall. Knees 90°. Hold isometric. Quad endurance. Minimal joint stress.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 30-90 sec holds. Noa: Safe, no eccentric damage. Boris: Finisher, muscle endurance.

exercises.name: Step-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Box height at knee. Step up, drive through heel. DBs at sides. Unilateral leg work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each leg. Noa: Safe functional movement. Boris: Moderate weight, volume.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Step backward into lunge. Front knee 90°. DBs at sides. Easier on knees than forward lunge.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps each leg. Noa: Knee-friendly lunge. Boris: Volume work.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
exercises.setupNotes: Longer stride for taller lifters. Alternate legs. DBs at sides. Functional leg work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 steps total. Noa: Safe unilateral. Boris: Conditioning element.

exercises.name: Sissy Squat
exercises.setupNotes: Lean back while squatting. Knees forward. Extreme quad stretch. Advanced movement.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-15 reps. Noa: High knee stress - careful. Boris: Quad finisher.

exercises.name: Good Morning
exercises.setupNotes: Bar on upper back. Hip hinge forward. Hamstring/glute/lower back. HIGH L4-L5 demand. Caution.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 8-12 reps. Noa: HIGH L4-L5 RISK - only if healthy. Boris: Accessory, light-moderate.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Arms on pad. Isolated bicep work. NO momentum. Can stress elbow in bottom stretch.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Elbow stress - avoid if injured. Boris: Strict form, peak contraction.

exercises.name: Concentration Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Seated, elbow against inner thigh. Isolated bicep. Slow tempo. Peak contraction focus.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Finisher, pump.

exercises.name: Incline Curl
exercises.setupNotes: 45° bench. Arms hang down. Greater bicep stretch. Supinate at top.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Moderate elbow stress. Boris: Long head emphasis.

exercises.name: Tricep Kickback
exercises.setupNotes: Bent over. Elbow high. Extend DB back. Squeeze tricep. Light weight, strict form.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Safe, low elbow stress. Boris: Finisher, pump.

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Up
exercises.setupNotes: Hands together forming diamond. Tricep emphasis. Harder than standard push-ups.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Moderate elbow stress. Boris: Bodyweight tricep work.

exercises.name: Bench Dips
exercises.setupNotes: Hands on bench behind. Feet on ground or elevated. Lower and press. Shoulder/elbow stress if too deep.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-20 reps. Noa: Monitor shoulder/elbow depth. Boris: High rep work.

exercises.name: Upright Row
exercises.setupNotes: Pull bar to chin. Elbows high. Traps/side delts. Can impinge shoulder - use wide grip.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 10-15 reps. Noa: Shoulder impingement risk - careful. Boris: Moderate weight.

exercises.name: Front Raise
exercises.setupNotes: DBs at thighs. Raise forward to shoulder height. Front delt isolation. Alternate or simultaneous.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 12-20 reps. Noa: Safe isolation. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Low pulley. Cable crosses body. Raise laterally. Constant tension. One arm at a time.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Safe, shoulder-friendly. Boris: Drop sets.

exercises.name: Reverse Pec Deck
exercises.setupNotes: Chest against pad. Arms wide. Squeeze rear delts. Posterior delt isolation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Safe, supported. Boris: High rep pump.

exercises.name: Adductor Machine
exercises.setupNotes: Legs on pads. Squeeze together. Inner thigh work. Safe isolation.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Safe, no knee stress. Boris: Accessory work.

exercises.name: Abductor Machine
exercises.setupNotes: Legs on pads. Push out. Outer hip/glute medius. Hip stability.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Hip health, injury prevention. Boris: Accessory work.

exercises.name: Donkey Calf Raise
exercises.setupNotes: Bent over, pad on lower back. Full calf stretch. High volume calf work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 20-40 reps. Noa: Safe, no spinal load. Boris: Pump work.

exercises.name: Wrist Curl
exercises.setupNotes: Forearms on bench. Wrists over edge. Curl DBs up. Grip/forearm work.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 15-25 reps. Noa: Forearm health. Boris: Accessory, grip strength.

exercises.name: Dead Hang
exercises.setupNotes: Hang from bar. Passive stretch. Shoulder decompression. Grip endurance.
exercises.committeeNotes: Dr. Brad: 30-90 sec holds. Noa: Shoulder health, spinal decompression. Boris: Every session.
